story.title: Three Businesses, One Building
story.subtitle: Broadie's, Harrison's, and Stauffer's Pharmacy
story.dek: A block-based exhibit shape inspired by Fortepan's FotoStory model: start with a folder of bookmarked photos, then order them into a narrative with contexts and connectors.
credits: Inspired by Fortepan US exhibit 25, One Building, Three Businesses
Demo data uses source links and simplified text for contract exploration

album.title: Broadie Building bookmark folder
album.description: A curated set of bookmarked photos and sources used to assemble the exhibit.

assets.title: The Broadie Building
assets.creator: Waverly Public Library / Fortepan IA
assets.caption: A Fortepan bookmark standing in for the hero image of the downtown Waverly building.

assets.title: Broadie's storefront
assets.creator: Waverly Public Library / Fortepan IA
assets.caption: The first business layer in the story: Broadie's drug, paint, and wallpaper business.

assets.title: Harrison's Five and Dime
assets.creator: Waverly Public Library / Fortepan IA
assets.caption: The second business layer: Harrison's expanded into the Broadie building.

assets.title: Stauffer's Pharmacy
assets.creator: Waverly Public Library / Fortepan IA
assets.caption: The third business layer: a pharmacy that kept the building in community memory.

contexts.title: Same storefront
contexts.body: The exhibit is organized around one downtown Waverly building rather than a simple photo chronology.

contexts.title: Bookmark folder
contexts.body: The album is the editorial starting point: selected assets are ordered into blocks and then connected with claims.

contexts.title: Community memory
contexts.body: Residents remember Harrison's as a place for candy, toys, gifts, and weekly shopping rituals.

contexts.title: Pharmacy memory
contexts.body: The pharmacy era connects the same building to prescriptions, soda fountain culture, and late-20th-century downtown commerce.

connectors.label: Same building, different business
connectors.body: The Broadie's and Harrison's blocks describe different business eras in the same storefront.

connectors.label: Broadie's gives way to Harrison's
connectors.body: The story needs an explicit relationship between business eras, not only chronological ordering.

connectors.label: Harrison's becomes Stauffer's Pharmacy
connectors.body: A later block can point back to the same place and explain the transition into pharmacy use.

connectors.label: Story derived from bookmark folder
connectors.body: The album is not the story; it is the curated source pile that the story orders and explains.

blocks.title: A storefront becomes a story container
blocks.body: This demo treats the exhibit as ordered content blocks over a bookmark folder. The same building anchors the narrative while business eras, memories, and source relationships provide the structure.

blocks.title: Broadie's: drugs, paint, wallpaper
blocks.body: The first section is an editorial chapter, not just an event. Its date range can feed a timeline projection, but the section order remains authorial.

blocks.title: A business anchors the block
blocks.body: Broadie's gives the story its opening business identity. The asset is linked to a place context and to later business-succession connectors.

blocks.title: Harrison's Five and Dime
blocks.body: The story shifts to a different business era in the same location. This is where connectors become more useful than flat chronological sorting.

blocks.title: The same building gains a new public memory
blocks.body: Harrison's changes the building's meaning from an earlier druggist storefront to a five-and-dime destination remembered through shopping rituals and downtown life.

blocks.title: Stauffer's Pharmacy
blocks.body: The final business layer keeps the same place visible while adding another use, another time range, and another set of memories.

blocks.title: A pharmacy chapter closes the sequence
blocks.body: The point is not just that Stauffer's happened later. The story records that it occupies the same narrative place and inherits the exhibit's accumulated context.

blocks.title: Timeline as projection, not source of truth
Credits and source trail
blocks.body: A Symfony UX Twig component should render this as stable HTML first, then allow JavaScript to hydrate the timeline strip, connector views, galleries, and other projections.
